title: AIMICI provides ScreenSkills accredited AI training for everyone in film and TV - ScreenSkills
text:
AIMICI provides ScreenSkills accredited AI training for everyone in film and TV
6 May 2026
ScreenSkills has worked with AIMICI to accredit a new suite of AI training courses designed for professionals across the screen industries, giving the workforce a credible, practical pathway to build AI literacy and confidence.
AI is already changing how productions are made. AIMICI's new courses, accredited by ScreenSkills and aligned with UK national AI skills standards, are designed for everyone working in film and television, regardless of role or technical background.
Rather than focusing on specific tools, which can quickly become outdated, the training builds lasting competencies and best practices for working with AI responsibly. The courses draw on guidance and frameworks from commissioners, broadcasters and unions, and align with national frameworks including those from The Alan Turing Institute and Skills England.
The courses have been developed in collaboration with Bournemouth University and UCL, drawing on leading academic research including the AHRC-funded report Shared-Posthuman Imagination: Human-AI Collaboration in Media Creation. In addition, more than 50 highly experienced professionals from across the screen industries including writers, directors, producers and VFX artists contributed their expertise during development to ensure the training reflects real-world practice.
The project has been supported by Innovate UK's BridgeAI programme, which promotes AI adoption across high-growth sectors in the UK.
Two courses, flexible options
The training is available as two standalone courses or as a bundle:
●
Level 1 — Responsible AI for Film & TV 101
(approx. 1 hour): Covers core AI literacy, risk and responsible use — equipping professionals with the essential knowledge to engage confidently with AI. No prior AI knowledge required. Includes interactive activities, short videos, quizzes and access to AIMICI's AI companion chatbot. A certificate is awarded on completion.
●
Level 2 — Responsible AI for Film & TV: Advanced Course
(approx. 4–5 hours):
Builds on Level 1 with practical use cases, feasibility assessments and real-world scenarios for applying AI in production contexts. Note: Level 1 is a prerequisite for Level 2.
Both courses are self-paced and structured into bite-sized 5–15 minute lessons, designed to fit around busy production schedules.
Pricing and availability
To access the courses you will need to activate or purchase a 30-day learning pass on AIMICI Platform. There are two types of passes available;
Starter Pass
: Access to the Level 1 course. Free for the ScreenSkills community for a limited time.
Use the discount code
SS-START-AI-FREE
at checkout.
Leader Pass
: Access to both the Level 1 & Level 2 courses (normally £75+VAT). If you are a ScreenSkills Trainee Finder trainee or other programme participant, you may be eligible for free or discounted passes, check with your programme manager for details.
Team pricing is available for production companies, studios and broadcasters. To organise team access please contact: sales@aimici.co.uk
Discover the training and take part
Notes on the accreditation
ScreenSkills are pleased to accredit AIMICI's new Introductory and Advanced courses in Responsible AI for Film & TV Production, having gone through a rigorous quality assurance process during the past 12 months during the courses' development. This included:
agreeing responsible AI learning objectives based on national standards in AI, and film and TV industry priorities
overseeing a thorough industry review and testing process for draft manuscripts and builds for the eLearning courses to ensure the courses are accessible and informative
close review to ensure learning objectives are sufficiently and clearly covered through content of modules
review against ScreenSkills' effective eLearning principles to demonstrate design and content of courses meets criteria to be flexible, accessible, authentic and active.
ScreenSkills' accreditation demonstrates that these courses meet industry-approved standards for understanding of responsible AI practice. ScreenSkills will continue to work with AIMICI to review the courses on a regular basis to ensure they can demonstrate ongoing accuracy and excellence as AI technologies and industry practices further evolve.
